channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Analysis of Competition Commission Remedies for Meta in South Africa
item.description: South Africa’s Competition Commission recently concluded its Media and Digital Platforms Market Inquiry (MDPMI), a significant step in regulating platform power in the Global South. While Google faced...

item.title: AI-Generated Fake Satellite Images Fuel Disinformation in US-Iran Conflict
item.description: As tensions escalate in the ongoing conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran, concerns are mounting over the rapid spread of disinformation amplified by artificial intelligence. An Irani...

item.title: Dangote Refinery and NNPCL Cause Petrol Prices to Spike in Abuja
item.description: NNPCL don increase petrol price again for Abuja, making am the second time inside less than 24 hours. As of Sunday, pump price don jump from ₦967 to ₦1,082 per litre after Dangote Refinery raise im ga...

item.title: Jaishankar Comments on Docking of Iranian Ships at Kochi Amid Iran-Israel Tensions
item.description: In the Indian Parliament, External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar addressed questions regarding the docking of Iranian ships, including the Iris Lavan, at Kochi. He clarified that the docking was in a...

item.title: Stephen Mayne reflects on Crikey’s journey and professionalisation in 2005
item.description: Crikey, the independent Australian news outlet, celebrates its 26th anniversary, marking two key milestones. The first is its official launch on 14 February 2000, during the dot-com boom. The second i...

item.title: Physiotherapist and paraplegic peer supporter deliver home-based wheelchair care and support in rural KZN
item.description: In the remote northern KwaZulu-Natal area near the Mozambique border, physiotherapist Maryke Bezuidenhout and paraplegic peer supporter Moses Mthembu form a dedicated outreach team from Manguzi Hospit...

item.title: PM Shehbaz rewards national hockey players for World Cup qualification
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Monday announced a reward of Rs1.5 million for each member of the national hockey team following their successful qualification for the FIH Hockey World Cup. This achi...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei takes over as Iran's supreme leader amid security-focused governance
item.description: Iran has officially seen a change in its top leadership as Mojtaba Khamenei succeeds his father, Ali Khamenei, as the supreme leader. Mojtaba is not a typical public figure; he has spent most of his c...

item.title: Flood survivors in Nairobi start slowly rebuilding homes and livelihoods
item.description: Victims of the recent floods in Nairobi have started to rebuild their lives after the heavy rains destroyed their homes and took away many of their belongings. The affected families, especially in inf...

item.title: IIT Roorkee Announces JEE Advanced 2026 Exam Date and Registration Schedule
item.description: IIT Roorkee has officially announced the schedule for the JEE Advanced 2026 examination. According to the latest notification, the exam is scheduled to be conducted on 17th May 2026. Students aspiring...

item.title: House Committee Approves Bill to Abolish Travel Tax
item.description: The House ways and means panel has approved a substitute bill aimed at abolishing the travel tax, moving the legislation one step closer to plenary debates. Authored by House Majority Leader Sandro Ma...

item.title: Heavy rains in Nairobi damage key water pipelines, causing supply interruptions
item.description: Residents in Nairobi are facing serious water supply interruptions after heavy rains caused significant flooding, damaging critical pipelines across the city. According to Nairobi City Water and Sewer...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei appointed Iran’s new supreme leader amid escalating Middle East conflict and rising oil prices
item.description: Iran has confirmed the appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, as its new supreme leader, following the death of his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in a recent airstrike. Mojtaba Khamenei, who has no pr...

item.title: Hungary Detains Ukrainian Bank Convoy Carrying $40M, €35M, and Gold, Launches Money Laundering Probe Amid Diplomatic Tensions
item.description: On March 5, 2026, Hungarian counter-terrorism forces (TEK) and customs authorities intercepted two armored cash-transport vehicles belonging to Ukraine's state-owned Oschadbank on the M-5 highway near...

item.title: FG Provides Emergency Contact Numbers for Nigerians in Middle East Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don release emergency contact numbers for all Nigerians wey dey live or dey travel for the Middle East because the situation for the region don dey tense due to confl...

item.title: Nigeria Emerges as Key Player in Renewable Energy Under President Tinubu
item.description: For the first time, Nigeria don start dey export solar PV panels, with Lagos-made panels dey go countries like Ghana. Dr Abba Aliyu, Managing Director of the Rural Electrification Agency (REA), talk s...

item.title: Prime Minister Carney announces April 13 byelections in Ontario and Quebec ridings
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has officially announced that byelections will take place on April 13 in three Canadian ridings: Scarborough Southwest and University—Rosedale in Ontario, and Terrebonne in ...

item.title: Noah Martin aims to secure second-row spot as Raiders adjust for Papali'i and Nicholson injuries
item.description: Canberra Raiders face selection adjustments ahead of their Round Two clash with the New Zealand Warriors after Josh Papali'i and Matty Nicholson were ruled out due to concussion and shoulder issues re...

item.title: Woman loses 35kg through structured eating habits after leukaemia recovery
item.description: Bernadette Lamaro, now 55, was diagnosed with leukaemia in July 2013 at the age of 42, just as her career in senior government HR was advancing. The diagnosis came after unexplained bleeding and bruis...

item.title: Tanzanian Doctor Brings Light-Based Therapy to Treat Chronic Illnesses
item.description: Dr Samson Magesa, a Tanzanian doctor, has started using phototherapy in Tanzania to help manage chronic diseases in a non-invasive and chemical-free way. After years of seeing patients struggle with c...
